title: Source lineage for The Destruction of Troy
summary: The pinned General Corpus XML is a copy of U-M CME encoding APE7380.0001.001, encoded from a complete TIFF facsimile of the collective Panton-Donaldson EETS edition. The archive ZIP independently contains the XML and TIFF layers in parallel branches. The edition transcribes the unique Hunter 388 witness of the Middle English translation-adaptation of Guido's Historia destructionis Troiae. Distinct 2002 diplomatic/color-facsimile and original 2010 critical editions by Hiroyuki Matsumoto provide supporting scholarly representations with separately recorded access and unresolved reuse rights.
review_notes: Primary chain: relation:001 -> relation:002 -> relation:005 -> relation:008 -> relation:010 -> relation:011.
Corrected parallel archive paths are relation:003 from the IA package to the XML encoding and relation:004 from the same package to the TIFF facsimile; neither branch passes through the other.
The target is printed pages 1-459 including inserted pages 12* and 13*, TIFFs 75-535, and witness folios 2r-214v; edition paratext and blank page 460 are outside the work boundary.
Glasgow's zero-canvas catalog record does not establish manuscript-image access. Appointment, enquiry, and publishing-request routes remain distinct.
For the 2002 offline edition, SEENET publisher-unavailable status, Glasgow-held carrier consultation, WorldCat discovery, volatile retailer access, and unknown reuse rights are independent facts.

The original 2010 critical edition remains distinct from the revised 2010 edition, 2010 concordance, 2011 third edition, and 2002 CD-ROM; its catalog/holdings route and dated non-orderable retailer routes do not establish full text or reuse rights.
No direct lineage edge to Benoît de Sainte-Maure's Roman de Troie is asserted.

entities.label: University of Glasgow Library, MS Hunter 388 (V.2.8)
entities.description: The unique known manuscript witness of The Destruction of Troy; the work occupies folios 2r-214v.

holding.notes: The current record exposes zero IIIF canvases; this is a catalog record, not a public manuscript-image sequence.

notes: The current record preserves an older one-scribe/two-styles description; MEC/LALME separately records two hands.
MEC/LALME records Hand A (LP 411) and Hand B (LP 23), both Lancashire; Glasgow preserves a historical one-scribe/two-styles wording. These claims remain separately attributed.
Nuthurst provenance is ownership history, not composition or copying location.

entities.label: The Destruction of Troy
entities.description: A single Middle English unrhymed alliterative romance translating and adapting Guido delle Colonne's Historia destructionis Troiae; represented by a prologue and thirty-six books and 14,044 encoded verse lines.

identifiers.scheme: Manual of the Writings in Middle English

notes: John Clerk of Whalley is the current attributed author at medium confidence; the translator/adapter role remains open.
The prior Sir Hugh of Eglinton/Huchown attribution is preserved as catalog history, not a current authorship assertion.

entities.label: Historia destructionis Troiae
entities.description: The Latin work by Guido delle Colonne named as the direct source of the Middle English translation-adaptation.
notes: No direct relation to Benoît de Sainte-Maure's Roman de Troie is asserted in this manifest.

evidence.citation: APE7380 raw XML/package audit, generated from the pinned XML and complete U-M raw package.
evidence.locator: fixity, member inventory, XML equivalence, TIFF integrity, and layer distinctions
evidence.summary: The audit verifies the pinned artifact fixity, complete package member inventory, normalized equivalence of the pinned and packaged XML, 670 TIFF payloads, and the distinction among package, encoding, and facsimile layers.
notes: Derived locally from access:ia-package and access:general-corpus-repository; its bytes were not downloaded from either input URL.
Input URLs are the exact source URLs recorded by those access routes.

evidence.citation: APE7380 target-boundary, page, and folio audit, generated from the pinned XML and complete U-M raw package.
evidence.locator: BODY DIV1 positions 2-38; pages 1-459; TIFFs 75-535; folio milestones 2r-214v
evidence.summary: The audit establishes the target selection, 37 representation sections, 249 LG groups, 14,044 L elements, exact printed-page/TIFF extent, Part I/II boundary, and observed manuscript folio milestones.
notes: Derived locally from access:ia-package and access:general-corpus-repository; its bytes were not downloaded from either input URL.
The prologue and thirty-six books are representation sections of one work, not 37 count-bearing works.

evidence.summary: The header supplies the encoding identifier, Middle English language, Panton-Donaldson edition description, 2006 electronic date, uncorrected OCR/TEI Level-1 statement, linked images, and legacy conditional copyright warning.

evidence.citation: Part I and Part II title pages in the complete U-M raw package for APE7380.0001.001.
evidence.locator: Part I and Part II title-page TIFFs
evidence.summary: The inspected title pages distinguish EETS Original Series 39 (1869) and 56 (1874) and support Panton's Part I and Donaldson's aggregate editorial responsibilities.
The API metadata identifies the complete U-M package filename, size, checksums, and archive item identity.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse item APE7380.0001.001.
evidence.summary: The normalized public item record supports the current endpoint, source-edition title and publication, Guido source, Troy subject, public-domain wording, and No Copyright - United States statement.
notes: The exact indexed item fields were preserved locally because automated publisher HTML retrieval encountered Cloudflare.

evidence.citation: Database of Middle English Romance, ‘Gest Historiale of the Destruction of Troy.’
evidence.summary: The record supports the work's title, date, Lancashire localization, unrhymed alliterative form, romance classification, Hunter 388 folios and copy date, Panton-Donaldson edition, and Guido source.

evidence.citation: Middle English Compendium Bibliography, BIB853, ‘Destruction of Troy.’
evidence.summary: The record supplies the work identifiers, Hunter 388 witness, two LALME hands, edition/facsimile bibliography, c1540 manuscript date, and questioned ?a1400 work-date stencil.
notes: Exact indexed record fields were preserved in the normalized local capture because automated publisher HTML retrieval encountered Cloudflare.

evidence.citation: University of Glasgow catalog record C/296758 for MS Hunter 388 (V.2.8).
evidence.summary: The current record supports the shelfmark, explicit John Clerk attribution, translated-from-Guido description, prior Huchown attribution history, historical scribal description, compressed Matsumoto publication note, and zero-canvas state.

evidence.citation: University of Glasgow catalog record C/908557 for the 2002 Matsumoto CD-ROM.
evidence.summary: The record identifies the extant held carrier as the same 2002 edition entity, shelfmark Sp Coll Hunterian Add. 283, and a zero-canvas catalog state.

evidence.citation: University of Glasgow Archives & Special Collections, ‘Book an appointment.’
evidence.summary: The page establishes the current onsite appointment route for consulting special-collections holdings.

evidence.citation: University of Glasgow Archives & Special Collections, ‘Enquiries.’
evidence.summary: The page establishes the current enquiry route for confirming access to Hunter 388 and the held Matsumoto carrier.

evidence.citation: University of Glasgow Archives & Special Collections, ‘Publishing requests.’
evidence.summary: The page supplies a publication/reproduction request route; it is not a permission grant or copyright-status statement.
The publisher listing identifies the 2002 Matsumoto edition and marks it ‘Not currently available.’

evidence.citation: CME repository LICENSE at commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c.
evidence.summary: The exact pinned repository root license is the Unlicense; its scope is kept on the repository distribution/artifact layer and is not propagated upstream.

evidence.citation: Thorlac Turville-Petre, ‘The Author of The Destruction of Troy,’ Medium Ævum 57.2 (1988), p. 264, public landing page.
evidence.summary: The public landing page confirms bibliographic identity and indexed John Clerk, authorship, translation, Whalley, Lancashire, Whalley Abbey, acrostic, and alliteration topics, but exposes neither an abstract nor article text.
notes: This evidence does not support the article's argument, reasoning, or conclusion details.

evidence.citation: CiNii Books record BB28693618 and exact retailer records for the original 2010 Matsumoto critical edition.
evidence.summary: The sources identify the original 2010 state, John Clerk attribution, Matsumoto editorship, ISBN 9784887309890, NCID BB28693618, Okayama publication, lx plus 534 pages and CD-ROM, holdings route, and current non-orderable retailer states.

evidence.citation: CiNii author aggregation and publication records distinguishing Matsumoto edition states.
evidence.summary: CiNii distinguishes the original and revised 2010 critical editions, a 2011 third edition, the 2010 lemmatized concordance, and the separate 2002 diplomatic/color-facsimile edition.
notes: The exact HMV item URL returned a provider error page and supplies no independent bibliographic fields.

evidence.citation: Arkeoloji ve Sanat retailer listing for ISBN 9780472002764.
evidence.summary: On 2026-07-17 the exact page displayed the 2002 edition, ISBN, price, stocktype=1, and an add-to-cart control.
notes: Fulfillment was not tested. This volatile retailer route is access evidence, not publisher availability, copyright status, or a reuse license.

open_questions.question: Should John Clerk receive a distinct translator and/or adapter role in addition to attributed author?
next_steps: Review Turville-Petre's full article or comparably direct scholarship before adding personal-role vocabulary.
notes: The work-level Guido translation-adaptation relation does not settle the personal role.

open_questions.question: How should DMER's 1350-1399 composition range and MEC's questioned ?a1400 value ultimately be normalized?
next_steps: Review a direct scholarly dating argument; retain both source-attributed values until then.

open_questions.question: How should MEC/LALME's two hands and Glasgow's historical one-scribe/two-styles description coexist?
next_steps: Consult a modern codicological study; until then preserve both claims with explicit attribution.

open_questions.question: Who controls reuse of the 2002 Matsumoto CD-ROM transcription and color facsimile, and is any lawful public copy available?
next_steps: Obtain a publisher or rights-holder statement while retaining held-copy consultation, publisher-unavailable status, and volatile retailer access separately.

open_questions.question: What reuse terms govern the original 2010 Matsumoto critical edition and accompanying CD, and did Glasgow's compressed 2010 citation intend the original or revised state?
next_steps: Obtain publisher or rights-holder terms for the original edition and CD.
Seek a copy-specific Glasgow or University Education Press record to resolve the compressed citation state.
